mission: Determine whether the residual Paper 35 surface route is fundamentally an operator problem or a state/representation problem on the physical puncture algebra.

theorem_1_current_surface_predictions_us.statement: On the current stack the natural physical surface state is the ABCK area-shell microcanonical state. It is tracial and puncture-symmetric, so it carries no visible-sector preference.

theorem_2_operator_only_selector_has_no_.statement: A symmetry-breaking selector B_sel on the same algebra does not by itself define a theorem-grade physical visible sector. Physical predictions require a state. Under the current symmetric tracial state, any label-independent observable extracted from B_sel is obtained by permutation averaging, and that averaged selector is equivariant. By the previous equivariant no-go, its induced visible charge must vanish.

imports.equivariant_boundary: No nontrivial visible-sector selector exists as a current-stack equivariant observable on the neutral puncture sector. The only surviving positive surface route is an explicitly symmetry-breaking selector B_sel with nonzero q.

theorem_3_exact_future_surface_object.statement: The remaining positive surface route is therefore not a bare operator B_sel. It is a nontracial, puncture-symmetry-breaking surface state or representation together with the induced nonzero charge. Equivalently, the exact future surface object is (omega_sb, q), or a representation-level refinement carrying the same data.

formulas.future_exact_object: (omega_sb, q) or equivalent symmetry-breaking representation datum
formulas.state_breaking: omega_sb o alpha_sigma != omega_sb for some sigma

final_boundary.safe_statement: The Paper 35 surface route is no longer an operator-selection problem. On the current algebra, any positive route requires a new nontracial symmetry-breaking surface state or representation datum, not a bare selector operator.
